_README: TERRY manager state file — the durable, single source of truth for the agent-fleet manager loop. JSON has no comments, so all guidance lives inside string values (this _README and the _model field). COPY THIS FILE to your project (e.g. tools/agent_queue.json), then: (1) replace example values, (2) keep ONE in_flight entry per running worker, (3) keep the queue priority-ordered (lowest prio number = highest priority), (4) keep done_this_session newest-first as a running ledger. The manager commits this file each heartbeat. Fields prefixed with _ are documentation/config; everything else is live state.
_model: MANAGER HEARTBEAT — run this loop each wake-up. (1) OBSERVE: read completion notifications; poll each in_flight worker's live state via its unique handle (HTTP status endpoint or bounded log tail — prefer structured polling over log-grep). Recognize and NO-OP stale completion echoes (a finished agent re-emits transcript tails that look like new events). (2) INTEGRATE: for each genuinely-completed worker, merge its branch into the integration branch; resolve conflicts deterministically (two distinct additions at one spot → keep BOTH; two competing fixes → keep the validated/more-complete one, drop the duplicate); run the test/verify suite IN THE MAIN checkout (NEVER the worktree — worktrees show spurious failures from incomplete fixtures); commit only if green. (3) DEQUEUE + DISPATCH: pop the next-priority (lowest prio) UNBLOCKED queue item; create a fresh worktree+branch off the CURRENT integration_head (git worktree add -f -b <branch> <wt> <integration-HEAD>); symlink heavy untracked deps (ln -sfn) and copy needed checkpoints as uniquely-named UNTRACKED files; assign a UNIQUE handle/port; dispatch exactly ONE worker with a prompt that states the single scoped blocker, the isolation contract (commit CODE ONLY to its branch; never touch integration branch/other worktrees), 'DRIVE ACTIVELY — do not arm a watcher and yield (that orphans the run and it dies)', a determinism bar (fixed/bounded budgets + two byte-identical output hashes), scoped process-kill hygiene (kill only its own handle/port), and a structured report-back contract (what worked + evidence, commit hashes, how far, next blocker). (4) RECORD: move completed workers to done_this_session (newest first), add the new in_flight entry, bump integration_head, update queue; commit this file. (5) RE-ARM: schedule the next heartbeat with a LONG fallback delay — completions wake you sooner, so the timer is only a safety net. RESOURCE WALL: cap concurrent HEAVY workers to machine capacity (watch free memory + load); a slot BLOCKED on another's fix should be PAUSED, not run into the same wall; give a LONG end-to-end completion run a quiet window. NEVER 'git clean' a worktree (it deletes symlinks + untracked checkpoints). Verify exactly one process per handle (kill orphans). Cross-check any 'impossible/unreachable' tooling verdict against ground truth before believing it. VERIFY BEFORE CLAIM: read the actual end state (memory/flag/output) before recording success.

_field_docs.integration_head: Short git SHA of the integration branch (the merge point NOTHING edits directly — only the manager merges into it). Bump after each successful merge+verify.
_field_docs.in_flight[].id: Stable slug for this run/blocker; matches the queue id it was dispatched from.
_field_docs.in_flight[].agent: Identifier of the dispatched worker agent (background task id / agent handle).
_field_docs.in_flight[].wt: Absolute or repo-relative worktree path for this worker (one worktree per worker).
_field_docs.in_flight[].branch: The worker's own branch (off current integration_head); worker commits CODE ONLY here.
_field_docs.in_flight[].port: Unique resource handle (e.g. --http-port NNNN, or a dir) so siblings never collide.
_field_docs.in_flight[].run: Logical run name (which pipeline/run-type this worker is advancing).
_field_docs.in_flight[].started: ISO timestamp the worker was dispatched (for staleness / resource-window decisions).
_field_docs.in_flight[].status: running | paused | awaiting-merge. Pause a slot genuinely BLOCKED on another's fix.
_field_docs.in_flight[].title: One line: what this worker is doing + its resume point + the immediate next step.
_field_docs.queue[].prio: Lower number = higher priority. Manager always dispatches the lowest-prio UNBLOCKED item next.
_field_docs.queue[].id: Stable slug; becomes in_flight[].id on dispatch.
_field_docs.queue[].blocked_by: Optional list of ids that must complete+merge first; keep PAUSED, do not dispatch yet.
_field_docs.queue[].title: The blocker / decision / hypothesis to be worked, stated as one scoped task.
_field_docs.done_this_session[]: Dated one-line ledger entries, NEWEST FIRST: merged commit shas, verify result, key decisions/conflict resolutions.
